feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un saluto informale perfetto per una festa.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, "Ciao! Come va?" significa Hi! How's it going?
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A perfect informal greeting for a party.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, "Ciao! Come va?" means Hi! How's it going?

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Si dice quando si conosce qualcuno di nuovo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, "Piacere di conoscerti!" significa Nice to meet you!
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! You say this when meeting someone new.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, "Piacere di conoscerti!" means Nice to meet you!

italian: Tu sei un amico di Paolo?
english: Are you a friend of Paolo's?
choicesItalian: Tu sei un amico di Paolo?
choicesEnglish: Are you a friend of Paolo's?
correctAnswer: Tu sei un amico di Paolo?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un ottimo modo per rompere il ghiaccio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi se è un amico di Paolo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A great way to break the ice.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking if they are a friend of Paolo's.

english: Do you want something to drink?
choicesEnglish: Do you want something to drink?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Molto gentile da parte tua.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, "Vuoi qualcosa da bere?" significa Do you want something to drink?
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Very kind of you.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, "Vuoi qualcosa da bere?" means Do you want something to drink?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Tipico di una festa affollata.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dici che c'è troppo rumore.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Typical of a crowded party.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're saying it's too noisy.

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Si parla spesso di musica alle feste.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi se gli piace la canzone.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! People often talk about music at parties.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking if they like the song.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una domanda comune per chi è appena arrivato.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi se conosce già tutti i presenti.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A common question for someone who just arrived.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking if they already know everyone there.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai cercando i drink.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A fundamental question.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're looking for the drinks.

choicesEnglish: Will you buy me a drink?
What do you want to eat?

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Un modo gentile per offrire da bere o da mangiare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, offri tu qualcosa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! A polite way to offer a drink or something to eat.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are offering something.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dici che la festa è bellissima.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A compliment for the organizer.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're saying the party is beautiful.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai presentando tua sorella.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! You are making introductions.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are introducing your sister.

english: Shall we go to the dance floor?
choicesEnglish: Shall we go to the dance floor?
Shall we go to the bar?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, proponi di andare a ballare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! An invitation to dance.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're suggesting going to dance.

english: Have you tried the mini pizzas?
choicesEnglish: Have you tried the mini pizzas?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Dal buffet, una domanda classica.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi se ha assaggiato le pizzette.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! From the buffet, a classic question.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking if they've tasted the mini pizzas.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Un modo enfatico per dire che il cibo è buono.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, "squisite" significa delicious.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! An emphatic way to say the food is good.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, "squisite" means delicious.

english: What are you having to drink?
choicesEnglish: What are you having to drink?
Have you already had a drink?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi cosa vuole bere.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! You are asking the other person's preference.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking what they want to drink.

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Stai ordinando o rispondendo a un'offerta.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, hai chiesto un gin tonic.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! You are ordering or responding to an offer.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you asked for a gin tonic.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi l'identità di un ragazzo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You're asking about someone.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking about a guy's identity.

english: He's an old friend of mine.
choicesEnglish: He's an old friend of mine.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Stai spiegando il tuo rapporto con lui.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dici che è un amico di vecchia data.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! You're explaining your relationship with him.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're saying he's a long-time friend.

english: How long have you known each other?
choicesEnglish: How long have you known each other?
Where did you see each other?

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Una domanda per approfondire la conoscenza.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi la durata della loro amicizia.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! A question to get to know more.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking about the duration of their friendship.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che vi conoscete da bambini.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Childhood friends.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means you've known each other since you were kids.

english: Excuse me, where is the bathroom?
choicesEnglish: Excuse me, where is the bathroom?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai cercando il bagno.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Essential logistical information.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're looking for the bathroom.

english: At the end of the hallway.
choicesEnglish: At the end of the hallway.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai indicando la fine del corridoio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! You are giving directions.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are pointing to the end of the hallway.

english: Do you come to these parties often?
choicesEnglish: Do you come to these parties often?
Do you go out often at night?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi della frequenza.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Classic small talk.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking about frequency.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dici che non sei mai venuto prima.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Direct answer.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're saying you've never come before.

english: What do you do for a living?
choicesEnglish: What do you do for a living?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi dell'occupazione.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! A classic topic for chatting.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking about their occupation.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Rispondi alla domanda sul lavoro.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, hai detto di essere un architetto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You're answering the question about your job.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you said you're an architect.

italian: E tu di cosa ti occupi?
choicesItalian: E tu di cosa ti occupi?
choicesEnglish: What do you do in your free time?
correctAnswer: E tu di cosa ti occupi?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Ricambi la domanda con cortesia.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi all'altro di cosa si occupa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! You're politely returning the question.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking the other person what they do.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dici che sei ancora uno studente universitario.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! You are a student.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're saying you're still a university student.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una reazione positiva a ciò che dice l'altro.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, "interessante" significa interesting.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A positive reaction to what the other person says.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, "interessante" means interesting.

english: Did you see what a beautiful cake?
choicesEnglish: Did you see what a beautiful cake?
Do you want a piece of cake?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, commenti la bellezza della torta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! An observation about the buffet.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're commenting on how beautiful the cake is.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dici che ha un buon aspetto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! An opinion based on appearance.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're saying it looks good.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, proponi di fare un brindisi.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A collective invitation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're suggesting making a toast.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, brindi in onore del festeggiato.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The toast's dedication.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're toasting in honor of the birthday boy.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si dice "cin cin".
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! The typical exclamation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you say "cin cin".

choicesEnglish: Can you pass me a napkin?
Can you give me a fork?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una richiesta pratica al buffet.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo un tovagliolo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A practical request at the buffet.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking for a napkin.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Rispondi cortesemente a una richiesta.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai porgendo l'oggetto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! You're politely responding to a request.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're handing over the object.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ringrazi con enfasi.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! Standard thank you.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're thanking with emphasis.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un modo per dire prego.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, "di nulla" è un modo per rispondere a grazie.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A way to say you're welcome.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, "di nulla" is a way to respond to thanks.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dici che il volume è eccessivo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A common complaint.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're saying the volume is too high.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, confermi la difficoltà a sentire l'altro.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! You're explaining why you don't understand.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're confirming the difficulty in hearing the other person.

choicesEnglish: Shall we go outside for a moment?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Per prendere aria o parlare meglio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, proponi di uscire all'aperto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! To get some air or talk better.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're suggesting going outside.

italian: Ottima idea, si soffoca qui dentro.
english: Great idea, it's stifling in here.
choicesItalian: Ottima idea, si soffoca qui dentro.
choicesEnglish: Great idea, it's stifling in here.
correctAnswer: Ottima idea, si soffoca qui dentro.

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Accetti la proposta con una motivazione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dici che dentro fa troppo caldo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! You accept the proposal with a reason.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're saying it's too hot inside.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi se si diverte.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A thoughtful question.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking if they're having fun.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! "Un sacco" è molto colloquiale e naturale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dici che ti diverti moltissimo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! "Un sacco" is very colloquial and natural.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're saying you're having a lot of fun.

italian: Che ne dici di un altro drink?

choicesEnglish: What do you want to eat?
correctAnswer: Che ne dici di un altro drink?

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Proponi di bere ancora qualcosa.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, proponi un secondo giro di drink.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! You're suggesting having another drink.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're suggesting a second round of drinks.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai dando il tuo consenso.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You are accepting the proposal.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are giving your consent.

choicesEnglish: I'm going to get a beer.

correctAnswer: Te ne prendo una anche a te?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti offri di prendere qualcosa anche per l'altro.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! A kind offer.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're offering to get something for the other person too.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un modo entusiasta di accettare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, "volentieri" significa gladly.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! An enthusiastic way to accept.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, "volentieri" means gladly.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dici che l'ora è avanzata.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A preamble for leaving.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're saying it's late.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dici che devi andartene.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! You are announcing your departure.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're saying you have to leave.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, esprimi sorpresa perché l'altro se ne va.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're expressing surprise that the other person is leaving.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dici che lavori presto l'indomani.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're saying you work early the next day.

english: It was a pleasure meeting you.
choicesEnglish: It was a pleasure meeting you.

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Un saluto formale ed elegante.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dici che ti ha fatto piacere la conoscenza.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! A formal and elegant greeting.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're saying you enjoyed meeting them.

choicesEnglish: For me too, see you soon!

feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You're returning the greeting.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're returning the pleasure and saying goodbye.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, proponi di sentirvi per messaggio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To stay in touch.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're suggesting talking via message.

choicesEnglish: No, I won't give it to you.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're giving your phone number.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're greeting the whole group.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, auguri un buon ritorno.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're wishing a good return.
